data.narrative:
Dawn Aerospace, a Series B space & aerospace company, has raised $30M+ to date. Valuation assessment draws on two applicable triangulation methods: stage-progression analysis and peer-median comparison within a comp universe of 3 same-sector, same-stage private companies (Aalyria, Hubble Network, Sift) and 5 public space & aerospace peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $30M raised proxy, yielding $57M. Peer-median valuation across the three private comparables yields $400M, with a range of $274M to $1.3B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median: 53.2x) is not applicable given the absence of target revenue disclosure.

The triangulated valuation range is $48M to $460M, with a median of $228M derived from both methods. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by the $1.3B outlier (Aalyria) within the peer set—reflects material uncertainty in comparable company selection and stage-specific pricing dynamics within frontier aerospace. Key sensitivities include revenue trajectory, technical de-risking milestones, and the applicability of early-stage aerospace valuations to this particular venture's commercial readiness and market timing.
